name: Caribbean Cabbage
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown pork steaks in butter, margarine, or oil. Set aside to cool.
2. Deglaze the skillet with 2 tablespoons water.
3. Pour chicken stock into a 4-quart pot.
4. Cut carrots into 1/8-inch thick slices and add them to the pot. Set the pot over medium heat.
5. Add chopped garlic salt, ground allspice, salt, and pepper to the pot.
6. Remove any bones from the pork steaks and cut them crosswise into 1/4-inch wide strips. Add the pork strips to the pot.
7. Core and coarsely chop the cabbage. Add it to the pot.
8. Stir well to mix all ingredients.
9. Cover the pot and allow the meat and vegetables to steam for a few minutes, but not too long. The carrots and cabbage should remain crisp (al dente) when served.
10. Serve with steamed rice.
OPTIONS:
- Alter spices as desired.
- Add, delete, or substitute any of the ingredients.
This dish is versatile, fast, easy, and inexpensive. It can feed four people for less than $5 and takes less than 20 minutes to prepare.
